LAW AND MOTION 302, A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT NATIONAL TENANT NETWORK, INC.'S SPECIAL DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S CLASS ACTION COMPLAINT - THE DEMURRERS ARE OVERRULED. THE COURT DOES NOT CONCLUDE AS A MATTER OF LAW THAT, ASSUMING THE TRUTH OF THE FACTUAL ALLEGATIONS, THERE IS "NO REASONABLE POSSIBILITY THAT THE REQUIREMENTS FOR CLASS CERTIFICATION WILL BE SATISFIED." TUCKER V. PACIFIC BELL MOBILE SERVICES, 208 CAL. APP. 4TH 201, 211 (2012). AS TO THE 2ND AND 3RD CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES THE ICCRA VIOLATIONS. AS TO THE 4TH AND 5TH CAUSES OF ACTION, PLAINTIFF HAS STANDING TO PURSUE THE UCL CAUSE OF ACTION, AND MAY BE ABLE TO OBTAIN INJUNCTIVE RELIEF. THESE CAUSES OF ACTION ARE PLED WITH SUFFICIENT PARTICULARITY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

LAW AND MOTION 302, A R G U E D; THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT NATIONAL TENANT NETWORK, INC.'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT IS DENIED, IN LIGHT OF AND FOR THE REASONS SET FORTH IN OVERRULING THE DEMURRER. THE COURT DOES NOT CONCLUDE AS A MATTER OF LAW THAT, ASSUMING THE TRUTH OF THE FACTUAL ALLEGATIONS, THERE IS "NO REASONABLE POSSIBILITY THAT THE REQUIREMENTS FOR CLASS CERTIFICATION WILL BE SATISFIED." TUCKER V. PACIFIC BELL MOBILE SERVICES, 208 CAL. APP. 4TH 201, 211 (2012). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; COURT REPORTER: ANTHONY C. VAUGHN, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

Defendant National Tenant Network, Inc.'S Motion To Strike Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-06-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KRISTEN G. SIMPLICIO, TEL. NO. (510) 325-4827 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. HEIDI ADAMS OF LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP, TEL. NO. 262-8507 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant National Tenant Network, Inc.'s Motion To Strike portions of Plaintiffs' Complaint is Denied, in light of and for the reasons set forth in overruling the demurrer. The Court does not conclude as a matter of law that, assuming the truth of the factual allegations, there is "no reasonable possibility that the requirements for class certification will be satisfied." Tucker v. Pacific Bell Mobile Services, 208 Cal. App. 4th 201, 211 (2012). ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)

Defendant National Tenant Network, Inc.'S Demurrer To Complaint
Off Calendar Mar-06-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. KRISTEN G. SIMPLICIO, TEL. NO. (510) 325-4827 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. HEIDI ADAMS OF LEWIS BRISBOIS BISGAARD & SMITH LLP, TEL. NO. 262-8507 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant National Tenant Network, Inc.'s Special Demurrer To Plaintiff's class action Complaint - The demurrers are overruled. The Court does not conclude as a matter of law that, assuming the truth of the factual allegations, there is "no reasonable possibility that the requirements for class certification will be satisfied." Tucker v. Pacific Bell Mobile Services, 208 Cal. App. 4th 201, 211 (2012). As to the 2nd and 3rd causes of action, Plaintiff sufficiently alleges the ICCRA violations. As to the 4th and 5th causes of action, plaintiff has standing to pursue the UCL cause of action, and may be able to obtain injunctive relief. These causes of action are pled with sufficient particularity. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =(302/MJM)
